generated_oml:
instance HokuyoUST10LXLidarSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST‑10LX LiDAR sensor mounted on the vehicle, produces raw LiDAR point‑cloud data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance D435iDepthCameraSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i depth camera, provides depth image data for obstacle detection"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraImageDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateECUSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle State ECU captures vehicle speed, steering angle, throttle, brake, and other dynamic states"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
]

instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingComponent : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking feature denoises, clusters, and tracks raw point‑cloud into labeled obstacles"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ObjectTrackDataTransmitted
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLidarSensor
]

instance DepthCameraImageDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom D435iDepthCameraSensor
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateECUSensor
]

instance ObjectTrackDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PointCloudClusteringTrackingComponent
]

instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Serialized and unit‑converted raw LiDAR point‑cloud data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler, PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler
]

instance DepthCameraImageData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Depth image data from the Intel Deepsense D435i camera"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraImageDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle speed, steering, throttle, brake, and other dynamic states"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Labeled obstacle tracks derived from clustered LiDAR point‑cloud"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler
]

instance ACCAlgorithmModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ACC algorithm that computes desired speed based on lead vehicle distance"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudComputationEnabler
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data Translation component (ISO 23247) serializes, aligns, and unit‑converts raw streams (LiDAR, depth, vehicle state) from the Physical Twin to the DT over Wi‑Fi (UDP)"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Pre‑processing feature (Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) denoises, clusters, and tracks LiDAR point‑cloud into labeled obstacles before visualization"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance CloudComputationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Computation offloading enabler that transfers the ACC algorithm to the cloud, computes desired speed, and streams the result back to the vehicle ECU"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance RemoteControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Remote Management enabler that receives low‑latency remote commands (enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, emergency brake) and forwards them to the vehicle ECU"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data Monitoring service displays real‑time vehicle state data, projected driving path, and detected object tracks; allows users to view, save, and generate reports"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight, ProjectedDrivingPathInsight, DetectedObjectTracksInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computation Offloading service transfers ACC algorithm to the DT (cloud), computes desired speed based on lead vehicle distance, and streams result back to the control unit"
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputeDesiredSpeedAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote Management service enables remote enable/disable of ACC, setting desired speed, and issuing emergency braking commands with low end‑to‑end latency, compliant with safety‑critical requirements"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, DisableACCAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

instance RealTimeVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides real‐time vehicle state data (speed, acceleration, steering, etc.)"
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPathInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Shows projected driving path for the vehicle"
]

instance DetectedObjectTracksInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Shows detected object tracks in the environment"
]

instance ComputeDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Computes desired speed for ACC based on lead vehicle distance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to enable ACC on the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DisableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to disable ACC on the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to set desired speed for ACC"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to initiate emergency braking"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control flows from the Digital Twin to the Physical Twin are issued through the Motor Actuation FE and Steering Servo Actuation FE components, which send actuation commands to the vehicle’s motors and steering servos. The message format, validation or authentication mechanisms, conflict‑resolution strategies, or command‑execution confirmation protocols are not described in the document."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin maintains a shared notion of time across its physical and digital layers, with a synchronization accuracy target of 100 ms to satisfy safety‑critical vehicular standards. This precision is mandated by hardware limitations and aligns with external timing standards that require sub‑100 ms coherence between physical devices and their digital replicas."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system follows the ISO 23247 reference architecture, mapping each software component to a Functional Element (FE) and implementing each FE as a separate ROS 2 package containing one or more ROS 2 nodes. The architecture partitions the twin into three primary entities—User, Digital Twin, and Physical Twin—each mapped to dedicated hardware components. The Digital Twin Entity houses data management, control logic, and high‑level analytics; the Physical Twin Entity encapsulates sensor interfaces, data pre‑processing, point‑cloud clustering, and actuation; the User Entity hosts the User Interface FE, split into visualization and control panels. This entity‑based, ROS 2‑centric orchestration allows for fine‑grained resource allocation and execution across heterogeneous compute nodes, while deviating from ISO 23247 by making the Physical Twin Entity host general automotive functionality beyond the standard’s scope."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Software components communicate internally via a ROS 2 middleware network, while inter‑entity communication between the Digital Twin and the Physical Twin occurs over a Wi‑Fi link using UDP as the transport protocol. The document does not specify the network topology, bandwidth, latency requirements, reliability or redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols for this communication."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The deployment architecture is illustrated in Figure 2, but the document contains no explicit details on hosting platforms (cloud/edge/on‑premise), computational or storage requirements, scalability mechanisms, supported deployment models, or availability/reliability assurance. Not in Document"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Compliance with ISO 23247 (Digital twin framework for manufacturing). The system follows the reference architecture defined in Part 2 of ISO 23247, comprising four entities: the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), the Data Collection and Device Control Entity, the Digital Twin Entity, and the User Entity, each of which is further subdivided into Functional Entities (FEs). While ISO 23247 does not prescribe specific implementation technologies, it encourages the use of standardized industrial protocols such as OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA). The provided implementation demonstrates the use of an OPC UA‑based data gateway in a GitHub repository (https://github.com/McSCert/Automotive-Digital-Twin) and a public demonstration video on YouTube (https://youtu.be/Rv-sJJQc3WU). The system therefore adheres to industry‑grade interoperability standards and indicates potential certification pathways through ISO 23247."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The design complies with ISO 26262‑1:2018 for functional safety of road vehicles, explicitly addressing the safety‑critical nature of the adaptive cruise control (ACC) subsystem. It also conforms to ISO 15622:2018, which specifies the performance requirements and test procedures for ACC systems. For V2X interoperability, the system follows the 3GPP TS 22.185 service‑requirements specification, which defines message latency limits, payload constraints and related service parameters for vehicular communications."
]
